description:
# Dispatch a multi-clause call statically using the pattern matrix

## Goal

Given a multi-clause `FnIr` and a tuple of input Descrs, select the
clause whose pattern matches, or report that no clause can be
statically selected (the Descr is too wide).

Uses the pattern compiler matrix from `fz-ul4.43` — does NOT
reinvent dispatch logic.

## What lands

```rust
pub enum Dispatch {
    /// Clause selected; the substituted bindings.
    MatchedClause { clause_idx: usize, bindings: HashMap<Var, Descr> },
    /// No clause statically matches (input is opaque).
    StopOpaque,
    /// Input is admissible but multiple clauses overlap statically
    /// (e.g. union of patterns) — caller may still inline if it
    /// emits a runtime test, but reducer treats as a stop in v1.
    StopAmbiguous,
}

pub fn dispatch(fn_ir: &FnIr, inputs: &[Descr]) -> Dispatch;
```

## Acceptance

- Unit tests on ast_eval's `eval` clauses:
  - `dispatch(eval, [{:num, 42}])` → `MatchedClause(0, {n: 42})`.
  - `dispatch(eval, [{:add, X, Y}])` → `MatchedClause(1, {a: X, b: Y})`.
  - `dispatch(eval, [any])` → `StopOpaque`.
  - `dispatch(eval, [int])` → `StopOpaque` (no clause matches a bare int).
- Same for `list_primitives` (length, map, foldl).
- Same for `mutual_recursion` (is_even/is_odd with int_lit vs int).
- No production caller yet.

## Removals

None.

## Depends on

`fz-ul4.43` (pattern matrix). The matrix is the dispatcher; this
ticket only adapts the matrix to consume a Descr tuple instead of
a runtime value.
title: fz-RED.2: clause dispatch via pattern matrix
